apps.name: Cloudflare Tunnel
apps.tagline: Expose local services to the internet securely via Cloudflare
apps.description: Cloudflare Tunnel creates a secure outbound connection from your server to Cloudflare's network — no inbound firewall rules or public IPs needed. Get your tunnel token from the Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard.
apps.compose:
services:
  cloudflared:
    image: cloudflare/cloudflared:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: tunnel --no-autoupdate run --token ${TUNNEL_TOKEN}

env.label: Tunnel Token
env.description: Found in Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard → Networks → Tunnels → your tunnel → Configure → Install connector.

apps.name: WireGuard Easy
apps.tagline: WireGuard VPN with a simple web UI
apps.description:
WG-Easy is the easiest way to run WireGuard VPN + a web UI for managing peers. Requires your server's public IP or hostname and a password hash.

Generate password hash on your server:
  docker run --rm ghcr.io/wg-easy/wg-easy:15 wgpw 'your-password'
apps.compose:
services:
  wg-easy:
    image: ghcr.io/wg-easy/wg-easy:15
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - WG_HOST=${WG_HOST}
      - WG_PORT=${WG_PORT}
      - WG_DEFAULT_DNS=${WG_DEFAULT_DNS}
    ports:
      - "${WG_PORT}:${WG_PORT}/udp"
      - "${WEBUI_PORT}:51821/tcp"
    volumes:
      - wg_data:/etc/wireguard
    cap_add:
      - NET_ADMIN
      - SYS_MODULE
    sysctls:
      - net.ipv4.conf.all.src_valid_mark=1
      - net.ipv4.ip_forward=1

volumes:
  wg_data:

env.label: Public Host
env.description: Public IP address or hostname of your server. Clients use this to connect.

env.label: WireGuard Port (UDP)
env.description: UDP port WireGuard listens on. Must be open in your firewall.

env.label: Web UI Port
env.description: Host port to expose the WG-Easy web UI on.

env.label: Default DNS
env.description: DNS server pushed to VPN clients.

apps.name: Beszel
apps.tagline: Lightweight server and Docker monitoring hub
apps.description: Beszel is a lightweight server monitoring platform with a web UI for tracking CPU, memory, disk, and Docker container stats across multiple systems.
apps.compose:
services:
  beszel:
    image: henrygd/beszel:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - APP_URL=${APP_URL}
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:8090"
    volumes:
      - beszel_data:/beszel_data

volumes:
  beszel_data:

env.label: App URL
env.description: The URL you will use to access Beszel (e.g. http://your-server-ip:8090 or your domain).

env.label: Port
env.description: Host port to expose the Beszel web UI on.

apps.name: MySpeed
apps.description: MySpeed saves your internet speed test results over time so you can track performance trends, detect outages, and monitor your ISP's reliability — all from a clean self-hosted dashboard.
apps.compose:
services:
  myspeed:
    image: germannewsmaker/myspeed:latest
    container_name: myspeed
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:5216"
    volumes:
      - myspeed_data:/myspeed/data

volumes:
  myspeed_data:

env.label: Port
env.description: Host port to expose the MySpeed web UI on.

apps.name: Uptime Kuma
apps.tagline: Self-hosted uptime monitoring tool with a fancy UI
apps.description: Uptime Kuma is an easy-to-use self-hosted monitoring tool for tracking uptime of websites, TCP ports, DNS, and more with notifications support.
apps.compose:
services:
  uptime-kuma:
    image: louislam/uptime-kuma:2
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:3001"
    volumes:
      - uptime_kuma_data:/app/data

volumes:
  uptime_kuma_data:

env.label: Port
env.description: Host port to expose the Uptime Kuma web UI on.
